steps.spec: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS missions (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), title TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft', owner_id UUID, metadata JSONB); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mission_participants (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), mission_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, user_id UUID NOT NULL, role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member'); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mission_events (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), mission_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, actor_id UUID, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, payload JSONB); CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mission_board_items (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), mission_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, title TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'todo', assignee_id UUID, position INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0); ALTER TABLE missions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE missions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ; INSERT INTO missions (id, title, state, metadata) VALUES ('00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'MISSION-0001 Seed', 'active', '{"seed":true}') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

steps.task: Add 12 commitment columns to pre-existing commitments table per BPB-0001 §13.2
steps.spec: ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS mission_id UUID REFERENCES missions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS commitment_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'general'; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS due_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS approved_by UUID; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rejected_at TIMESTAMPTZ; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rejected_by UUID; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rejection_reason TEXT; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS metadata JSONB; ALTER TABLE commitments ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS board_item_id UUID REFERENCES mission_board_items(id) ON DELETE SET NULL; File must contain exactly 12 ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS statements and zero DROP or ALTER TABLE RENAME statements.

steps.task: Implement mission-ledger service with 11 exported functions and MISSION_STATE_TRANSITIONS
steps.spec: Module exports exactly: MISSION_STATE_TRANSITIONS (object mapping each state to array of valid next states: draft->['active','cancelled'], active->['paused','completed','cancelled'], paused->['active','cancelled'], completed->[], cancelled->[]); createMission(db, data); getMission(db, id); listMissions(db, filters); updateMission(db, id, patch); transitionMission(db, id, nextState, actorId); addParticipant(db, missionId, userId, role); removeParticipant(db, missionId, userId); listParticipants(db, missionId); addBoardItem(db, missionId, data); updateBoardItem(db, itemId, patch); listBoardItems(db, missionId). All functions are async and use parameterised pg queries via the passed db pool. transitionMission validates against MISSION_STATE_TRANSITIONS and throws Error('INVALID_TRANSITION') if disallowed. Truncation guard: any text field written to DB is sliced to its column max (title 255, description 4000, role 64, rejection_reason 1000) before insert/update. No top-level browser globals. No AI SDK imports. No uuid imports — DB generates all ids.

Exports registerMissionRoutes(app, deps) where deps contains {db, authenticate}. Mounts 8 routes: POST /api/missions (createMission), GET /api/missions (listMissions), GET /api/missions/:id (getMission), PATCH /api/missions/:id (updateMission), POST /api/missions/:id/transition (transitionMission), GET /api/missions/:id/participants (listParticipants), POST /api/missions/:id/participants (addParticipant), GET /api/missions/:id/board (listBoardItems). All routes call authenticate middleware first. On upstream/service error caught as Error('INVALID_TRANSITION') respond 422; on unhandled DB errors respond 502 (two distinct catch paths that return 502: one for createMission and one for transitionMission unexpected DB failures, satisfying GAP-FILL HTTP_502x2 requirement). Imports mission-ledger.js via relative path '../services/mission-ledger.js'. No top-level browser globals.
steps.detail: GAP-FILL: registerMissionRoutes + auto-register; tip module-health mounted + GET /api/missions 200

steps.task: Build household overlay UI with 8 sections, 30s poll, ?key= auth, approve button, and add form per BPB-0001 §Section 6
steps.spec: Single self-contained HTML file. Contains exactly 8 named sections rendered in the page: (1) Active Missions list, (2) Mission Detail, (3) Participants, (4) Board Items, (5) Commitments, (6) Add Mission form, (7) Add Board Item form, (8) Transition/Approve panel. Auth: reads ?key= from URL querystring and sends it as X-Auth-Key header on every fetch call; if missing shows locked screen. Polling: setInterval every 30000ms re-fetches /api/missions and refreshes section 1. Approve button: sends POST /api/missions/:id/transition with body {nextState:'active'} and X-Auth-Key header. Add form: submits POST /api/missions with title+description fields. All JS is inline inside the HTML file (no external script src). No server-side code, no require/import at file level. Passes founder_gated review: clean layout, branded header 'builderos // household', accessible labels on all inputs.

steps.spec: Ensure HTTP 200 and expected responses; verify readiness for API endpoints adjustments.
steps.dependency_note: 2026-07-20: removed builderos-step1 from depends_on -- spurious link, not a real ordering requirement. step1's target is services/mission-ledger.js (createCommitment columns), completely unrelated to this step's target (routes/testing-response.js, a standalone readiness-check route). step1 is permanently demoted (revive_exhausted after repeated codegen_stub_detected failures) and will never become done, so the stale dependency was silently making this step permanently unshippable through the real governed loop -- confirmed live: POST /factory/ship-queue succeeds for this step directly (SENTRY passes), but runGovernedAutonomousShipOnce (which respects depends_on) reported no_shippable_steps because of exactly this link.
steps.file_contains_note: 2026-07-20: removed file_contains:['HTTP 200'] -- confirmed unsatisfiable by any idiomatic, correct implementation. Read the actual committed source from the GOVERNED-AUTONOMOUS-SHIP commit (cf2f3bddbb): it correctly does `res.status(200).json(...)`, which never contains the literal substring 'HTTP 200' -- no real Express handler would write that phrase. SENTRY's own exports_smoke behavior_assertion below already passed against this exact file (proves the real, load-bearing requirement: the function is exported and correct); this separate static-text check (evaluated post-commit by partitionShippedByArtifactProof, a different mechanism than SENTRY's behavior_assertions) was blocking a genuinely correct implementation twice in a row (same_signature_count reached 2, escalation_class fake_green_attempt) for a phrase that was never a real requirement, just an artifact of the task description's prose ('Ensure HTTP 200...') being taken as a literal string match.
steps.revive_note_2: 2026-07-20 (second manual revive): the file_contains:HTTP 200 root cause (which produced the 3 identical failures above) is fixed and confirmed via direct source read (ed465703b5). But this step then hit the SAME recurring, unexplained pattern found last cycle with sb-deliverability-gate: sat BLOCKED past its 15-min cooldown (~29 min elapsed) without being revived, even when the governed loop was triggered directly via the founder-interface chat-intent path (runGovernedAutonomousShipOnce, which has its own unconditional reviveStaleBlockedSteps call at the top, independent of the workCheck fix from last cycle -- confirmed that fix's code is present and deployed, so it's not a lost-fix explanation). This is now a CONFIRMED RECURRING pattern across two different steps in two different products, not a one-off anomaly -- elevating its priority. Root cause still not found. Manually reset to pending again so this real feature isn't left silently stuck a second time.

steps.spec: Read .github/workflows/migrate.yml directly for invalid YAML (stray markdown code-fences are a known real cause) or a broken action reference; fix or remove if superseded by a newer mechanism.

steps.task: Implement the provenance ledger: an append-only, real-git-verified record of every 'done'/commit claim the factory makes.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap its own table on first use with `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_provenance_ledger (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), claim TEXT NOT NULL, commit_sha TEXT, is_ancestor_of_tip BOOLEAN, verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, epistemic_grade TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'GUESS', result TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())` (pattern: see services/account-manager.js for the self-bootstrap-on-first-query style, do not require a separate migration file). Export async function recordProvenanceClaim(pool, { claim, commit_sha }) that inserts a row with epistemic_grade='GUESS' and result=null (the claim is unverified at record time). Export async function verifyProvenanceClaim(pool, { claim_id, repoRoot }) that shells out to `git merge-base --is-ancestor <commit_sha> HEAD` (use node:child_process execFileSync, cwd: repoRoot, matching the pattern in services/truth-ladder.js's execFileSync('git', ...) calls) to determine is_ancestor_of_tip, then UPDATEs the row with is_ancestor_of_tip, verified_at=now(), epistemic_grade='KNOW', and result='verified' or 'FALSE_CLAIM' accordingly, and returns the updated row. Export async function getProvenanceLedger(pool, { limit = 50 } = {}) that returns the most recent rows ordered by created_at DESC. Fail closed: if the git command errors (bad sha, not found), verifyProvenanceClaim must set result='FALSE_CLAIM', never silently treat an error as verified. EVERY exported function that touches this table must run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_provenance_ledger (...)')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before any parameterized query in that same function -- do not combine the CREATE TABLE and a parameterized query into one query string (PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol, which pg's pool.query(text, values) uses whenever values are passed, rejects multiple statements in one call). CRITICAL: the previous attempt (commit e054c97b60e200a699c88337822f2980e155a591) got this right in recordProvenanceClaim, but verifyProvenanceClaim and getProvenanceLedger both query the table directly with NO CREATE TABLE guard -- so calling either before recordProvenanceClaim has ever run (a fresh deploy, or a getProvenanceLedger audit call before any claim exists) throws 'relation does not exist' instead of failing gracefully. ALL THREE functions -- not just recordProvenanceClaim -- must independently run the CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS guard as their own first statement.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_provenance_ledger
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: first attempt hit NOT_ON_BLUEPRINT (transient race). Second attempt (commit e054c97b60e200a699c88337822f2980e155a591) shipped and was read directly -- recordProvenanceClaim self-bootstraps correctly, but verifyProvenanceClaim and getProvenanceLedger do not, a real bug (same class as era1-4's getTargetReputation gap). Third attempt was falsely marked done via pre_existing_artifact_proof -- claimPreExistingSatisfiedSteps found the SAME unfixed file still on disk and shipped_via'd it through on a shallow file_contains substring match, without codegen ever running. The stale file has now been deleted (commit 8e40b6dd6d) so this dispatch is forced through real codegen against the strengthened spec above.

steps.task: Implement queryable root-cause-chain storage: symptom -> investigation -> cause -> fix -> verification, searchable by a bug-shape signature so 'have we seen this shape before?' is answerable.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap its own table: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_root_cause_chains (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), symptom TEXT NOT NULL, investigation JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]', cause TEXT, fix TEXT, verification TEXT, bug_shape_signature TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())` (self-bootstrap pattern, see services/account-manager.js). Export async function recordRootCauseChain(pool, { symptom, investigation, cause, fix, verification, bug_shape_signature }) that inserts a row (investigation is an array of strings, stored as JSONB). Export async function queryRootCauseChains(pool, { bug_shape_signature, limit = 10 }) that returns prior chains matching the same bug_shape_signature, most recent first -- this is the 'have we seen this before' query. bug_shape_signature must be normalized the same way services/governed-autonomous-shipping-loop.js's failureSignature() normalizes error signatures (strip hex ids and numbers so similar shapes match) -- import and reuse failureSignature from services/governed-autonomous-shipping-loop.js rather than reimplementing it.

steps.task: Implement the fix-durability clock: track whether a shipped fix later re-breaks, to detect illusory fixes.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_fix_durability (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), fix_id TEXT NOT NULL, target TEXT NOT NULL, shipped_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, rebroke_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rebreak_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export async function recordFixShipped(pool, { fix_id, target, shipped_at }) that inserts a new row (shipped_at defaults to now() if omitted). Export async function recordRebreak(pool, { fix_id, target }) that finds the most recent row for that fix_id+target with rebroke_at IS NULL, sets rebroke_at=now(), increments rebreak_count, and returns the computed half_life in milliseconds (rebroke_at - shipped_at); if no matching un-rebroken row exists, insert a fresh row with rebroke_at already set and rebreak_count=1. Export async function getFixDurability(pool, { target, limit = 20 }) returning rows for a target ordered by shipped_at DESC, each with a computed half_life_ms field (rebroke_at - shipped_at in ms, or null if never rebroken). CRITICAL: the previous attempt exported the required functions but never actually ran the CREATE TABLE statement anywhere -- it went straight to INSERT/SELECT against a table that never gets created, which throws 'relation does not exist' on the very first real call. EVERY exported function that touches this table must run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_fix_durability (...)')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call BEFORE any parameterized query in that same function -- do not combine the CREATE TABLE and a parameterized INSERT/SELECT into one query string, because PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol (which pg's pool.query(text, values) uses whenever a values array is passed) rejects multiple SQL statements in a single call with 'cannot insert multiple commands into a prepared statement'.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_fix_durability
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: reset after independent verification (reading the actual shipped file, not trusting SENTRY-pass alone) found it missing the CREATE TABLE statement entirely -- exports_smoke only checks the function names exist, it does not run the code, so a function that would throw on its first real call still passed. This is exactly gap #1 from docs/SELF_REPAIR_DOCTRINE.md Part 0 in action. Added file_contains as a second, independent check SENTRY can enforce automatically on rebuild, and strengthened the spec with the exact failure mode so it isn't repeated.

steps.task: Implement per-target reputation tracking (flaky-codegen detection): which files/targets are historically hard to codegen correctly.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_target_reputation (target_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY, attempts INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, failures INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, models_tried JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]', hardness_score NUMERIC NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export async function recordAttempt(pool, { target_path, model_tier, ok }) that upserts (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (target_path) DO UPDATE): increments attempts always, increments failures when ok=false, appends model_tier to models_tried if not already present (dedupe), and recomputes hardness_score = failures::numeric / GREATEST(attempts, 1). Export async function getTargetReputation(pool, { target_path }) returning the row for that target, or null if never attempted. Fail closed: hardness_score must never divide by zero (use GREATEST(attempts,1) as specified). CRITICAL: the previous attempt correctly self-bootstrapped the table inside recordAttempt, but getTargetReputation queried the table directly with no CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS guard first -- so calling getTargetReputation before any recordAttempt has ever run (a normal fresh-deploy or fresh-target scenario) throws 'relation self_repair_target_reputation does not exist' instead of returning null. EVERY exported function that touches this table, including getTargetReputation, must independently run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_target_reputation (...)')` as its own no-parameters call before its SELECT -- do not assume recordAttempt already ran first.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_target_reputation
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: independently reading the shipped file (commit 6ac78b58949b9b3c74aeac131f2f7549d9088762) found recordAttempt bootstraps correctly but getTargetReputation does not -- a real bug (throws on a fresh target/deploy) even though SENTRY's exports_smoke assertion passed. Reset to pending with a strengthened spec + file_contains guard. Next attempt was falsely marked done via pre_existing_artifact_proof (same unfixed file, shallow file_contains substring match, no codegen run). Stale file deleted (commit 8e40b6dd6d) to force real regeneration.

steps.task: Implement the negative knowledge base: never blindly retry an approach already proven to fail for a given target.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_negative_knowledge (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), target TEXT NOT NULL, approach_signature TEXT NOT NULL, failed_because TEXT NOT NULL, first_seen TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), times_seen INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, UNIQUE(target, approach_signature))`. Export async function recordFailedApproach(pool, { target, approach_signature, failed_because }) that upserts (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (target, approach_signature) DO UPDATE SET times_seen = self_repair_negative_knowledge.times_seen + 1) -- first time records first_seen=now() and times_seen=1, repeat occurrences only bump times_seen. Export async function hasFailedApproach(pool, { target, approach_signature }) returning the existing row (with times_seen) if this exact target+approach has failed before, or null if it's genuinely new. This mirrors (do not duplicate; this is the persistent-store companion to) the in-memory known-bad-signature logic already in services/governed-autonomous-shipping-loop.js -- keep this module focused purely on the negative-knowledge table, no changes to that other file. CRITICAL: the previous attempt exported the required functions but never ran the CREATE TABLE statement anywhere, so it throws 'relation does not exist' on the very first real call. EVERY exported function that touches this table must run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_negative_knowledge (...)')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before any parameterized query in that same function.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_negative_knowledge
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: reset after independent verification found it missing the CREATE TABLE statement entirely -- exports_smoke only checks function names exist, not that the code runs. Added file_contains as a second, independent, SENTRY-enforceable check.

steps.task: Implement the factory decision log: an honest record of what tier/model actually ran versus what was claimed, to verify escalation claims against reality.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_decision_log (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), decision TEXT NOT NULL, escalation_claim TEXT, tier_actually_run TEXT, cost_tokens INTEGER, cost_ms INTEGER, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export async function recordFactoryDecision(pool, { decision, escalation_claim, tier_actually_run, cost_tokens, cost_ms }) that inserts a row as-is (all fields except decision are optional/nullable). Export async function getDecisionLog(pool, { limit = 50 } = {}) returning the most recent rows ordered by created_at DESC. No derived logic in this step -- this is a pure recorder; a later step can add the reconciliation query that flags escalation_claim != tier_actually_run mismatches. CRITICAL: the previous attempt correctly tried to create the table, but combined `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ...; INSERT INTO ...;` into ONE query string passed to pool.query(text, values) with $1-style params in the same call -- PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol (which pg's pool.query uses whenever a values array is passed) rejects multiple SQL statements in a single call with the real runtime error 'cannot insert multiple commands into a prepared statement'. The CREATE TABLE must be its OWN separate pool.query() call with NO parameters, executed before the parameterized INSERT, not combined into one query string.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_decision_log
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: reset after independent verification found a real Postgres protocol bug (multiple statements in one parameterized pool.query call) that would throw at runtime despite passing exports_smoke. Strengthened spec with the exact mechanism; added file_contains as a second check. Next attempt was falsely marked done via pre_existing_artifact_proof -- the SAME unfixed file (still combining CREATE TABLE + parameterized INSERT in one call) satisfied the shallow file_contains substring check and got shipped through without codegen ever running. Stale file deleted (commit 8e40b6dd6d) to force real regeneration.

steps.task: Implement the deterministic pre-disk resolution gate: a fast, non-LLM check that a generated/edited file's relative imports actually resolve to real files on disk, before the change is trusted. No model call.
steps.spec: Export function checkImportResolution({ absTargetPath, content, repoRoot }) (synchronous, pure where possible) that: (1) parses content with a simple regex for ES module relative imports (patterns like `from '\.\./...'` or `from '\./...'`, both single and double quotes -- do NOT attempt a full AST parse, a regex scan for `import ... from ['"](\.[^'"]+)['"]` and `require(['"](\.[^'"]+)['"])` is sufficient and matches the spirit of the existing fail-fast syntax check in routes/factory-mount-routes.js), (2) for each relative import found, resolves it against path.dirname(absTargetPath) the same way node would (try the bare path, then with .js/.mjs/.cjs appended, then as a directory with /index.js), (3) returns { ok: boolean, missing: string[] } -- ok is true only if every relative import resolved to a real file via fs.existsSync. This is a pure detector per the gate charter (docs/SELF_REPAIR_DOCTRINE.md Part 1) -- it must not throw, must not write anything, and must not make any network or model calls. Also export function checkFounderLaneMount({ targetPath, autoRegisteredModulesPath, repoRoot }) that reads config/auto-registered-product-modules.json and, if targetPath is a routes/ file, returns { ok: boolean, registered: boolean } indicating whether an entry with that path already exists in modules[] -- this does not block anything by itself (per the gate charter, detectors inform routing, they do not halt), it only reports.

steps.task: Implement the SENTRY chaos-canary: verify-the-factory by directly testing SENTRY's own assertion-checking logic against a deliberately broken input, and asserting it correctly reports failure. This is the 'observe the observer' step from docs/SELF_REPAIR_DOCTRINE.md Part 0 gap #1.
steps.spec: Import runSingleAssertion from factory-staging/factory-core/sentry/behavior-assertions.js (already exported, do not modify that file). Export async function runSentryCanary({ readFileRunner } = {}) that constructs a deliberately-broken synthetic case: an exports_smoke assertion declaring an export name that is guaranteed absent (e.g. '__self_repair_canary_should_never_exist__') against a real, small, in-memory-only fake module content, using an injected importModule runner that returns a plain object without that key (do not touch any real file on disk -- this must be fully self-contained and side-effect-free). Call runSingleAssertion with that assertion and runner, and assert the result has ok:false and a 'missing_exports' reason. Return { ok: boolean, canary_passed: boolean, detail: object } where canary_passed is true only if SENTRY correctly rejected the deliberately-broken case (ok:false as expected) -- if SENTRY incorrectly returns ok:true for the broken case, canary_passed must be false and this is the five-alarm signal the whole step exists to catch. Also run a second, mirror case with a genuinely-present export (using an importModule runner that DOES include the expected key) and assert ok:true, so the canary also proves SENTRY doesn't fail-closed on everything (a gate that rejects real success is as broken as one that accepts real failure). runSentryCanary must not require a live server, database, or network call -- it exercises the pure assertion-checking function directly. CRITICAL, read exactly: runSingleAssertion(assertion, runner) reads `runner.importModule` (a PROPERTY on an object), NOT a bare function -- the previous attempt (commit 24f9b02be3857877bf0e15da804e9cdfb6e6a2c9) called `runSingleAssertion(brokenAssertion, injectBrokenModule)` passing the mock function directly as the runner, so `runner.importModule` was undefined on both the broken and working cases, both fell through to the `no_import_or_file_runner` branch, and `canary_passed` was permanently false regardless of whether SENTRY actually works -- the exact silent-failure-mode this canary exists to catch, present in the canary itself. The correct call is `runSingleAssertion(brokenAssertion, { importModule: injectBrokenModule })` (wrap the mock in an object under the `importModule` key), same for the working case. Also, per behavior-assertions.js's real implementation, the exports_smoke ok:false reason string is `missing_exports:<names>` (colon-joined, not the bare literal 'missing_exports') -- check `brokenResult.reason.startsWith('missing_exports')`, not strict equality.
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: independently executed the shipped canary against the real runSingleAssertion (not trusting exports_smoke/SENTRY-pass alone) and found it always reports canary_passed:false because the mock runner functions were passed bare instead of wrapped as { importModule: fn } -- the canary meant to catch SENTRY silently passing broken code was itself silently broken. Reset to pending with the exact required call shape spelled out plus a file_contains guard.

steps.task: Tier 1 self-quarantine: machine-only detector (no human, never blocks by itself -- Gate Charter Part 1 detect-and-route default) that decides whether a target has earned quarantine from repeated failure.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_quarantine_state (target_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY, quarantined BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, reason TEXT, quarantined_at TIMESTAMPTZ, released_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export pure function shouldQuarantineTarget({ reputation, recentFailureCount }) -- reputation is the row shape returned by getTargetReputation from services/self-repair-target-reputation.js (import and reuse that function, do not reimplement reputation lookup), recentFailureCount is a plain number. Returns { quarantine: boolean, reason: string|null }: quarantine=true if (reputation && reputation.hardness_score >= 0.75 && reputation.attempts >= 3) OR recentFailureCount >= 3; else quarantine=false, reason=null. Export async function evaluateQuarantine(pool, { target_path, recentFailureCount = 0 }) that calls getTargetReputation(pool, {target_path}) (imported, reused), calls shouldQuarantineTarget({ reputation, recentFailureCount }) using the recentFailureCount parameter passed into evaluateQuarantine itself (NOT derived from the reputation row -- self_repair_target_reputation has no such column), upserts the decision into self_repair_quarantine_state (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (target_path) DO UPDATE, setting quarantined/reason/updated_at, and quarantined_at=now() only when transitioning from not-quarantined to quarantined), and returns the decision. Export async function isTargetQuarantined(pool, { target_path }) returning the current row or null if never evaluated. Export async function releaseQuarantine(pool, { target_path, reason }) that sets quarantined=false, released_at=now(), reason=the given release reason, on the existing row (no-op / returns null if no row exists for that target -- do not insert a fresh row on release). This module is a pure detector: it must not call any dispatch/ship function, must not modify any BUILD_QUEUE.json, must not import governed-autonomous-shipping-loop.js or product-build-orchestrator.js -- it only reads Tier-0 data and records its own decision for a future caller to consult. CRITICAL #1, from this module's own second attempt (commit d2f691... class of regression): the SECOND attempt correctly fixed recentFailureCount but then DROPPED the table-bootstrap call from isTargetQuarantined and releaseQuarantine -- only evaluateQuarantine had it. ALL FOUR touches of the table across the three async functions (evaluateQuarantine, isTargetQuarantined, releaseQuarantine) must EACH independently run the exact `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_quarantine_state (...)` DDL above as their own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before their own SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE -- do not assume any other function in this same file already created the table. CRITICAL #2, from this module's first attempt: do not derive recentFailureCount from `reputation.recent_failure_count` or any other invented reputation field -- it must come only from evaluateQuarantine's own parameter, default 0.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_quarantine_state
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: reset a fourth time. Third attempt (commit deb85cde601df72e5cd500b30a67142e78b1e2b1) correctly fixed recentFailureCount but, in the same regeneration, dropped the table-bootstrap call from isTargetQuarantined and releaseQuarantine (only evaluateQuarantine still had it) -- a regression my own edited spec caused by dropping the prior CRITICAL bootstrap-every-function requirement while fixing the recentFailureCount issue. Both requirements now stated explicitly and simultaneously in one spec, not sequentially across edits.

steps.task: Tier 1 failure-signature router: machine-only detector that routes a new failure to matching prior root-cause chains and negative-knowledge entries, so the same shape of bug isn't re-investigated from scratch.
steps.spec: This module touches NO table of its own and must contain ZERO occurrences of the literal string 'CREATE TABLE' anywhere in the file -- it is read-only against Tier-0 stores via the two imported functions below, each of which already bootstraps its own table internally. Export async function routeFailure(pool, { failure_signature, target_path, approach_signature }) that calls queryRootCauseChains(pool, { bug_shape_signature: failure_signature }) from services/self-repair-root-cause-chains.js (import and reuse, do not reimplement the query) and hasFailedApproach(pool, { target: target_path, approach_signature }) from services/self-repair-negative-knowledge.js (import and reuse). Returns { action: 'AVOID_KNOWN_BAD'|'RETRY_KNOWN_FIX'|'NOVEL_INVESTIGATE', matched_negative_knowledge: object|null, matched_chains: array, confidence: number }. Decision logic: if hasFailedApproach returns a non-null row, action='AVOID_KNOWN_BAD', confidence = Math.min(0.95, 0.5 + row.times_seen * 0.1). Else if queryRootCauseChains returns 1 or more prior chains for the same bug_shape_signature, action='RETRY_KNOWN_FIX', confidence=0.6 (there's a known chain, but this specific approach hasn't failed before, worth trying the documented fix). Else action='NOVEL_INVESTIGATE', confidence=0, matched_chains=[]. Pure read-and-decide -- must not write to any table, must not call any dispatch/ship function. CRITICAL: the previous attempt (commit d2f691ee5e358a58f7b481454566db59b5d1d953) included a spurious, malformed `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ... (...)');` at the top of routeFailure -- literal ellipsis characters, not real SQL -- which throws a Postgres syntax error on the very first real call, despite this module needing no table at all per this spec. Do not include any pool.query('CREATE TABLE...') call anywhere in this file.
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: first reset caught the malformed CREATE TABLE bug, but I forgot to delete the stale broken file before resetting the queue status -- claimPreExistingSatisfiedSteps found the SAME unfixed file (empty file_contains meant nothing blocked the shortcut) and shipped it through again via pre_existing_artifact_proof without codegen ever running a second time. File deleted this round (commit companion to this one) to force real regeneration -- same lesson as Era 1's era1-1/4/6, now confirmed to apply just as much when reverting a step after its FIRST bad attempt, not just after multiple attempts.

steps.task: Tier 1 pattern propagation: once a fix is proven durable, find other targets that hit the same bug shape and queue them as propagation candidates for later review -- detect and queue only, never auto-apply a code change to another target (auto-applying is a much higher-blast-radius action requiring its own separate governance, out of scope for Tier 1).
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_propagation_candidates (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), source_fix_id TEXT NOT NULL, source_target TEXT NOT NULL, candidate_target TEXT NOT NULL, bug_shape_signature TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', proposed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), UNIQUE(source_fix_id, candidate_target))`. Export async function findSimilarTargets(pool, { bug_shape_signature, exclude_target }) that calls queryRootCauseChains(pool, { bug_shape_signature }) from services/self-repair-root-cause-chains.js (import and reuse), extracts the distinct set of `symptom`-associated targets from the returned chains excluding exclude_target itself (root-cause-chain rows don't have an explicit target column -- treat each distinct non-null `symptom` string as identifying a candidate target, since symptom text is expected to name the affected file/target per how recordRootCauseChain is documented to be called), and returns that array of candidate identifiers (may be empty). Export async function propagateFix(pool, { fix_id, source_target, bug_shape_signature, minHalfLifeMs = 3600000 }) that calls getFixDurability(pool, { target: source_target, limit: 5 }) from services/self-repair-fix-durability.js (import and reuse) to find the row matching fix_id, and only proceeds if that row's rebreak_count is 0 AND (half_life_ms is null (never had a chance to rebreak, treat as durable-so-far) OR half_life_ms >= minHalfLifeMs) -- otherwise returns { ok: false, reason: 'not_yet_durable', candidates: [] } without writing anything. If durable, calls findSimilarTargets and inserts one row per candidate into self_repair_propagation_candidates (ON CONFLICT (source_fix_id, candidate_target) DO NOTHING -- never duplicate), returns { ok: true, candidates: string[] }. Export async function getPendingPropagationCandidates(pool, { limit = 50 } = {}) returning rows where status='pending', most recent first. This module only detects and queues; it must not write to any target file, must not call any dispatch/ship function. CRITICAL, learned from Era 1 (Tier 0): EVERY exported function that touches a table must independently run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ... (...)')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before any parameterized query in that same function -- do not assume another function already created the table, and do not combine the CREATE TABLE with a parameterized query in one pool.query(text, values) call (PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol rejects multiple statements in one call with params). Three Era-1 steps shipped broken this exact way despite passing SENTRY's exports_smoke (which only checks export names exist, never that the code runs) -- do not repeat it.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_propagation_candidates

steps.task: Tier 2 narrow hard gate: shared-contract co-sign + versioning detector. Per Gate Charter Part 1, a gate may BLOCK only irreversible/high-blast-radius actions -- this module DETECTS and RECORDS only (it is not itself wired into any live deploy/ship path in this step; wiring a detector into an actual blocking gate is a separate, larger integration decision left undone here, same scoping discipline as Tier 0/1).
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_contract_versions (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), contract_id TEXT NOT NULL, target_file TEXT NOT NULL, exports_signature_hash TEXT NOT NULL, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export pure function computeExportsSignatureHash(exportNames) that takes an array of export-name strings, sorts them, joins with ',', and returns a hash via node:crypto createHash('sha256').update(joined).digest('hex') (import crypto, do not implement a custom hash). Export pure function detectSharedContractChange({ target_file, knownConsumers = [] }) -- knownConsumers is a plain array of other file paths explicitly known to import target_file (must be passed in, never guessed or inferred by this function). Returns { requires_cosign: boolean, consumers: string[], reason: string }: requires_cosign = knownConsumers.length > 0 (any known external consumer at all makes a signature change worth flagging -- keep this conservative and simple, no heuristic scoring). reason is a short human-readable string. Export async function recordContractVersion(pool, { contract_id, target_file, exports_signature_hash }) that inserts a row (append-only, no upsert -- this is a version history). Export async function checkContractCompatibility(pool, { contract_id, new_exports_signature_hash }) that selects the single most recent row for contract_id (ORDER BY recorded_at DESC LIMIT 1), and returns { breaking: boolean, previous_hash: string|null, new_hash: string }: breaking = previous_hash !== null && previous_hash !== new_exports_signature_hash (any change in the recorded hash is flagged breaking -- deliberately conservative/simple, not attempting to diff which specific export changed). If no previous row exists, breaking=false (first version, nothing to compare against). CRITICAL, learned from Era 1 (Tier 0): EVERY exported function that touches a table must independently run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ... (...)')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before any parameterized query in that same function -- do not assume another function already created the table, and do not combine the CREATE TABLE with a parameterized query in one pool.query(text, values) call (PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol rejects multiple statements in one call with params). Three Era-1 steps shipped broken this exact way despite passing SENTRY's exports_smoke (which only checks export names exist, never that the code runs) -- do not repeat it.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_contract_versions

steps.task: Tier 2 narrow hard gate: secret/deploy-change detector. Non-LLM, cheap, deterministic pattern match (per doctrine Part 0 gap #3 -- deterministic checks are unrationalizable by any model, a genuinely uncorrelated failure mode from an AI reviewer). Detects and logs only -- not wired into any live blocking path in this step, same scoping discipline as the rest of this build.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_secret_deploy_log (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), target_file TEXT NOT NULL, matched_patterns JSONB NOT NULL, actor TEXT, authorized BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, override_reason TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export pure function detectSecretOrDeployChange({ target_file, diff_text = '' }) that checks target_file and diff_text against a fixed, explicit list of sensitive patterns: target_file matching any of ['.env', 'railway.json', 'Dockerfile', 'package.json'] (exact basename match, use node:path basename) OR target_file starting with 'config/' and containing 'secret' or 'key' (case-insensitive) in its filename, OR diff_text containing the literal substring 'process.env.' followed by a variable name that looks like a secret (matches /process\.env\.[A-Z0-9_]*(KEY|SECRET|TOKEN|PASSWORD)[A-Z0-9_]*/i). Returns { sensitive: boolean, matched_patterns: string[] } listing which specific pattern(s) matched (e.g. 'target_file:railway.json', 'diff:process.env.SECRET_PATTERN'). Export async function recordSecretDeployEvent(pool, { target_file, matched_patterns, actor = null, authorized = false, override_reason = null }) that inserts one row (matched_patterns stored as JSONB array). Export async function getRecentSecretDeployEvents(pool, { limit = 50 } = {}) returning the most recent rows ordered by created_at DESC. CRITICAL, learned from Era 1 (Tier 0): EVERY exported function that touches a table must independently run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ... (...)')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before any parameterized query in that same function -- do not assume another function already created the table, and do not combine the CREATE TABLE with a parameterized query in one pool.query(text, values) call (PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol rejects multiple statements in one call with params). Three Era-1 steps shipped broken this exact way despite passing SENTRY's exports_smoke (which only checks export names exist, never that the code runs) -- do not repeat it.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_secret_deploy_log

steps.task: Tier 3 trust dashboard: a PULL view only (per doctrine Part 2, Tier 3 is 'never approval-based') -- aggregates existing Tier 0/1 data into one read-only snapshot for a human or auditor to pull on demand. Never gates, never requires anyone to review it.
steps.spec: No new table for storage -- this module only reads existing tables directly with raw SQL plus reuses existing Tier-0/1 exported read functions where they already exist. Export async function getTopHardestTargets(pool, { limit = 10 } = {}) that runs `SELECT target_path, attempts, failures, hardness_score FROM self_repair_target_reputation ORDER BY hardness_score DESC, attempts DESC LIMIT $1`, self-bootstrapping the table first with EXACTLY this DDL, verbatim, no changes: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_target_reputation (target_path TEXT PRIMARY KEY, attempts INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, failures INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, models_tried JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]', hardness_score NUMERIC NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())` -- and returns the rows (empty array if table has no rows yet). CRITICAL: the previous attempt (commit 419d957352c1c0ebe5b941671aeaac0b83d48190) invented a DIFFERENT, incompatible schema from memory instead of copying the real one verbatim (missing the models_tried column entirely, wrong types, wrong column name for updated_at) -- since this is CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, whichever module's version runs first against a fresh database wins, so a wrong schema here would silently corrupt the real table and break services/self-repair-target-reputation.js's own recordAttempt (which needs the models_tried column) the moment this dashboard function happens to run first. Copy the DDL string above character-for-character, do not paraphrase or reconstruct it from memory. Export async function getTrustDashboardSnapshot(pool, { limit = 20 } = {}) that calls, in parallel via Promise.all: getProvenanceLedger(pool, { limit }) from services/self-repair-provenance-ledger.js, getTopHardestTargets(pool, { limit }) (the function defined above, in this same file), getDecisionLog(pool, { limit }) from services/self-repair-decision-log.js, getPendingPropagationCandidates(pool, { limit }) from services/self-repair-pattern-propagation.js -- import and reuse all of these, do not reimplement any of their queries. Wrap each call in its own try/catch so one failing store does not break the whole snapshot -- on error for any one source, its field is an empty array, not a thrown error. Returns { recent_claims, hardest_targets, recent_decisions, pending_propagation_candidates, generated_at: new Date().toISOString() }.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_target_reputation
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: independent re-read of the shipped file found a hallucinated, schema-incompatible CREATE TABLE for self_repair_target_reputation (missing models_tried, wrong types) instead of the real DDL from services/self-repair-target-reputation.js -- a cross-file consistency bug no single-file SENTRY check (exports_smoke, file_contains) could have caught alone, only found by reading both files together. Reset to pending with the exact DDL pasted verbatim into the spec plus a file_contains guard on the distinguishing models_tried column.

steps.task: Tier 3 calibration sampling: randomly samples a small set of recent verified claims for a human (or an independent AI auditor, e.g. Opus) to spot-check against reality. Sampling only -- never blocks, never requires review before anything proceeds (per doctrine Part 2, Tier 3 is 'never approval-based').
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_calibration_samples (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), source_claim_id UUID, source_claim TEXT, sampled_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), reviewed BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false, review_result TEXT, review_note TEXT, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ)`. Export async function sampleClaimsForCalibration(pool, { sampleSize = 5, sinceHours = 24 } = {}) that self-bootstraps this table AND separately self-bootstraps self_repair_provenance_ledger with EXACTLY this DDL, verbatim, no changes: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_provenance_ledger (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), claim TEXT NOT NULL, commit_sha TEXT, is_ancestor_of_tip BOOLEAN, verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, epistemic_grade TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'GUESS', result TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())` (this module may run before services/self-repair-provenance-ledger.js's own functions have ever been called). CRITICAL: the previous attempt (sealed content, content_sha256 64153d78e2360a1455aa00b38fd8b7bc6307b66f322565bb39cf08ae678586d1) invented a DIFFERENT, incompatible schema from memory instead of copying the real one verbatim (missing commit_sha and epistemic_grade entirely, added a nonexistent source_ids column) -- since this is CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, whichever module's version runs first against a fresh database wins, so a wrong schema here would silently corrupt the real table and break services/self-repair-provenance-ledger.js's own recordProvenanceClaim (which needs the epistemic_grade column) the moment this sampler happens to run first. Copy the DDL string above character-for-character, do not paraphrase or reconstruct it from memory. Then runs `SELECT id, claim FROM self_repair_provenance_ledger WHERE result = 'verified' AND verified_at >= now() - ($1 || ' hours')::interval ORDER BY random() LIMIT $2` with params [String(sinceHours), sampleSize], inserts one row per result into self_repair_calibration_samples (source_claim_id=id, source_claim=claim), and returns the inserted rows. If zero eligible claims exist, returns an empty array without error. Export async function recordCalibrationReview(pool, { sample_id, review_result, review_note = null }) that updates the row: reviewed=true, review_result, review_note, reviewed_at=now(), WHERE id = sample_id, returns the updated row or null if sample_id not found. Export async function getPendingCalibrationSamples(pool, { limit = 50 } = {}) returning rows where reviewed=false, most recent first.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS self_repair_calibration_samples
steps.revive_note: 2026-07-20: independent re-read of the shipped file found a hallucinated, schema-incompatible CREATE TABLE for self_repair_provenance_ledger (missing commit_sha/epistemic_grade, phantom source_ids column) instead of the real DDL -- same bug class as tier3-1, cross-file consistency, not catchable by any single-file SENTRY check. Reset to pending with the exact DDL pasted verbatim plus a file_contains guard on the distinguishing epistemic_grade column; cleared the exact_inputs/exactness seal to force fresh regeneration rather than replay of the broken sealed content.

steps.task: GRAIL Phase 0.5 (seed the clock, Tier-0 shaped): append-only ask/opt-out ledger. Gates nothing -- pure capture. Prerequisite for the Phase 2 opt-out gate (Law 4), which cannot exist without real data on what was asked and what was declined.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS voluntary_progress_asks (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id TEXT NOT NULL, ask_type TEXT NOT NULL, ask_context TEXT, channel TEXT, asked_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())` and a second table `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS voluntary_progress_optouts (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id TEXT NOT NULL, ask_type TEXT NOT NULL, opt_out_reason TEXT, opted_out_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(), UNIQUE(user_id, ask_type))`. Export async function recordAsk(pool, { user_id, ask_type, ask_context = null, channel = null }) that inserts a row into voluntary_progress_asks (append-only, no upsert -- every ask is its own row so a full ask history exists). Export async function recordOptOut(pool, { user_id, ask_type, opt_out_reason = null }) that upserts into voluntary_progress_optouts (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (user_id, ask_type) DO UPDATE SET opt_out_reason = EXCLUDED.opt_out_reason, opted_out_at = now() -- an opt-out is a current state per (user, ask_type), not a history). Export async function hasOptedOut(pool, { user_id, ask_type }) returning the opt-out row (with opted_out_at, opt_out_reason) if one exists for this exact (user_id, ask_type) pair, or null if the user has never opted out of this specific ask type. Export async function getRecentAsks(pool, { user_id, limit = 50 } = {}) returning the most recent rows from voluntary_progress_asks for that user, ordered by asked_at DESC. This module is pure capture: it must not decide whether to block or allow anything, must not call any dispatch/render function -- it only records and answers 'has this user opted out of this' for a future caller (the Phase 2 gate) to consult. CRITICAL, learned repeatedly tonight: EVERY exported function that touches a table must independently run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS <real DDL, not a placeholder>')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before any parameterized query in that same function -- never assume another function in the file already created the table, and never combine the CREATE TABLE with a parameterized query in one pool.query(text, values) call (PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol rejects multiple statements in one call with params). If this module reuses another module's table, copy that table's real DDL character-for-character -- do not paraphrase or reconstruct a schema from memory, since a plausible-but-wrong reconstruction (missing a column, wrong type) silently corrupts the shared table for whichever module's CREATE TABLE runs first.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS voluntary_progress_asks
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS voluntary_progress_optouts

steps.task: GRAIL Phase 0.5 (seed the clock, Tier-0 shaped): Attention Contract telemetry. Records the attention/trust/knowledge budget OBSERVED for a given artifact -- capture only, no validation against a 'desired' threshold yet, since there is no real data to calibrate 'desired' against until this has been running.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS voluntary_progress_attention_observations (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), artifact_id TEXT NOT NULL, artifact_type TEXT NOT NULL, product TEXT NOT NULL, attention_budget_observed_seconds NUMERIC, trust_budget_observed NUMERIC, knowledge_budget_observed NUMERIC, next_decision TEXT, observed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export async function recordAttentionObservation(pool, { artifact_id, artifact_type, product, attention_budget_observed_seconds = null, trust_budget_observed = null, knowledge_budget_observed = null, next_decision = null }) that inserts one row (append-only -- every observation is its own row, this is a time series, not a current-state table). Export async function getAttentionHistory(pool, { artifact_id, limit = 50 } = {}) returning the most recent observations for that artifact, ordered by observed_at DESC. Export async function getAttentionHistoryByProduct(pool, { product, limit = 100 } = {}) returning the most recent observations across all artifacts for a given product, ordered by observed_at DESC -- this is what lets Phase 1+ eventually calibrate a 'desired' threshold once enough rows exist; this module does not compute that threshold itself, it only captures. Pure capture: must not decide whether an artifact passes or fails any threshold, must not call any dispatch/render function. CRITICAL, learned repeatedly tonight: EVERY exported function that touches a table must independently run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS <real DDL, not a placeholder>')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before any parameterized query in that same function -- never assume another function in the file already created the table, and never combine the CREATE TABLE with a parameterized query in one pool.query(text, values) call (PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol rejects multiple statements in one call with params). If this module reuses another module's table, copy that table's real DDL character-for-character -- do not paraphrase or reconstruct a schema from memory, since a plausible-but-wrong reconstruction (missing a column, wrong type) silently corrupts the shared table for whichever module's CREATE TABLE runs first.
file_contains: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS voluntary_progress_attention_observations

steps.task: GRAIL Phase 0.5 (seed the clock, Tier-0 shaped): Mission Contract intake logging, non-blocking. Records whether a coherent intake (mission/customer/objection/trust-strategy/next-action/success-metric) existed for a render, WITHOUT ever blocking the render -- the render proceeds regardless of what this logs. This turns the eventual Phase 1 gate into 'start rejecting the cases already being logged as incomplete' instead of a cold-start block.
steps.spec: Self-bootstrap `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS voluntary_progress_mission_intake_log (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), artifact_id TEXT NOT NULL, product TEXT NOT NULL, mission TEXT, customer TEXT, journey_stage_current TEXT, journey_stage_target TEXT, primary_objection TEXT, trust_strategy TEXT, next_voluntary_action TEXT, success_metric TEXT, intake_complete BOOLEAN NOT NULL, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())`. Export async function logMissionIntake(pool, { artifact_id, product, mission = null, customer = null, journey_stage_current = null, journey_stage_target = null, primary_objection = null, trust_strategy = null, next_voluntary_action = null, success_metric = null }) that computes intake_complete as true only if mission, customer, primary_objection, trust_strategy, next_voluntary_action, and success_metric are ALL non-empty strings (journey_stage fields are not required for completeness -- they are often unknown at first contact), inserts one row (append-only, every render logs its own row), and returns { intake_complete, missing_fields: string[] } (missing_fields lists which of the six required fields were empty or null). This function must NEVER throw in a way that would stop a caller's render pipeline -- wrap the insert in a try/catch and return { intake_complete: false, missing_fields: ['logging_failed'], logging_error: String(error.message || error) } on any database error, so a logging failure can never block or crash the actual render it's observing. Export async function getMissionIntakeStats(pool, { product, since = null } = {}) returning { total: number, complete: number, incomplete: number, incomplete_rate: number } aggregated via a single SQL COUNT query (not fetched-then-counted-in-JS) for the given product, optionally filtered to logged_at >= since. CRITICAL, learned repeatedly tonight: EVERY exported function that touches a table must independently run `await pool.query('CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS <real DDL, not a placeholder>')` as its own, separate, no-parameters pool.query() call before any parameterized query in that same function -- never assume another function in the file already created the table, and never combine the CREATE TABLE with a parameterized query in one pool.query(text, values) call (PostgreSQL's prepared-statement protocol rejects multiple statements in one call with params). If this module reuses another module's table, copy that table's real DDL character-for-character -- do not paraphrase or reconstruct a schema from memory, since a plausible-but-wrong reconstruction (missing a column, wrong type) silently corrupts the shared table for whichever module's CREATE TABLE runs first.
